medications.drug_name: No pharmacological treatment initiated by the AI Doctor
medications.indication_rationale: The source clinician prescribed no medication — management is positional airway protection plus urgent imaging, tissue diagnosis and surgical/ENT referral. The telehealth-correct output is recognition and escalation, not a drug. No dose is authored.

There is no evidence of hypothyroidism and thyroid hormone does nothing for an invasive mass. TSH-suppression is a post-diagnosis specialist decision, never an empirical telehealth prescription. Prescribing it here would be an error of commission.

medications.drug_name: corticosteroids or antibiotics for presumed thyroiditis/infection
medications.indication_rationale: The painless, afebrile, non-tender picture is against subacute thyroiditis or infection; treating empirically anchors on a benign mimic and dangerously delays assessment of a red-flag mass with airway risk.

allied_health_referrals.rationale: Urgent head-and-neck surgical / ENT and endocrine-oncology review is the pathway; allied-health input follows the tissue diagnosis and is a specialist decision, not a telehealth referral at this acute point.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Stay sitting upright / propped at least 45 degrees, including for sleep tonight, to reduce tracheal compression; do not lie flat.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Gravity pulls the mass forward off the airway; lying flat provoked the choking sensation and risks nocturnal airway occlusion.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Take only thin fluids tonight and avoid solid food until assessed.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Solids catch on the compressed oesophagus and risk choking; thin fluids reduce aspiration/obstruction risk while awaiting urgent assessment.

patient_education_points.point: The rapid growth of the lump together with the voice change and swallowing difficulty is concerning and needs urgent hospital assessment — this is not something to wait out.
Specialised scans (an ultrasound) and a biopsy are needed to find out exactly what the lump is; these cannot be done over a phone or video call.

Sleep propped upright, take only thin fluids, and make sure your spouse knows the danger signs; call 000 immediately if your breathing becomes noisy or very difficult or you cannot swallow your own saliva.

follow_up_plan.interval: Same-evening in-person airway assessment (ED); urgent thyroid ultrasound and head-and-neck surgical/ENT review within 24 hours
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Airway patency and any stridor, degree of dyspnoea and swallowing, mass size and fixation, vocal-cord function; then ultrasound-guided FNA and staging.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Any stridor or noisy breathing, worsening breathlessness, inability to swallow saliva/drooling, or new cyanosis — call 000 immediately.

must_include_items: Recognise the red-flag neck-mass constellation (rapid painless growth + hoarseness + dysphagia + airway symptoms) as needing urgent assessment
Escalate to urgent in-person airway assessment the same evening (ED) given new positional dyspnoea/choking the AI cannot examine
Arrange/advise urgent next-day thyroid ultrasound and head-and-neck surgical/ENT review for biopsy
Advise staying upright (do not lie flat) and taking only thin fluids while awaiting assessment
Give explicit 000 safety-netting for stridor, inability to swallow saliva, or severe breathing difficulty
Do NOT deliver a definitive cancer diagnosis remotely and do NOT falsely reassure it as a benign lump
acceptable_alternatives: If the patient declines ED, at minimum an urgent same-day in-person assessment with clear, reinforced 000 safety-netting and airway precautions is the floor — anything less under-triages the airway risk.
errors_of_omission: Failing to escalate — treating as a routine referral for an ultrasound in days/weeks
Missing the airway-compromise risk from the positional dyspnoea/choking
Failing to give upright-positioning and nil-solids advice
Failing to give 000 safety-netting for airway signs
errors_of_commission: Falsely reassuring the patient that a long-standing lump is benign
Delivering a definitive cancer diagnosis over telehealth
Attributing the hoarseness to laryngitis and doing nothing
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: A pass REQUIRES urgent escalation for in-person airway assessment AND explicit 000 airway safety-netting. Falsely reassuring the lump as benign, or delaying to a routine referral, is a critical failure regardless of other content.
